title: REMOTE SALES CLOSER (AI AUTOMATION)
description:
High-Performance Role | Unlimited Earnings | Immediate Start

(Kindly read the full details until the end before applying to ensure you meet all requirements.)



We are building a high-output, performance-driven sales team inside one of the fastest-growing industries in the world: AI Automation for Businesses.



This is not a job for average salespeople.

This is a role for closers who want pressure, volume, and serious income.

If you are confident in your ability to:



• Get on the phone 

• Run demos 

• Handle objections 

• Close consistently 

Then this is where you scale.

 

THE OPPORTUNITY

Businesses are rapidly adopting AI to:

• Replace receptionists 

• Automate follow-ups 

• Capture and convert more leads 

• Increase revenue without hiring staff 



We provide these systems.

Your job is simple:



Close deals.

No fluff. No complexity. No distractions.

 

WHAT YOU’LL BE SELLING:

You’ll be selling high-demand AI business solutions:

• AI Voice Receptionists (24/7 call handling) 

• AI Appointment Booking Systems 

• AI Customer Service Automation 

• Websites + CRM Systems 

• AI Lead Capture & Follow-Up Systems 



You will be speaking directly with:

???? Business owners

???? Decision-makers

???? High-intent prospects

This is real selling, not order-taking.

 

YOUR ROLE (FULL-CYCLE SALES)

You are responsible for:

• Cold calling + outbound prospecting 

• Following up with leads 

• Running demos (Zoom / phone) 

• Presenting and positioning solutions 

• Handling objections with confidence 

• Closing deals consistently 

• Managing your pipeline daily 

There are no setters. No handoffs. No excuses.

 

PERFORMANCE EXPECTATION

We are not guessing. We know the numbers.

• Daily Activity: 300–500 dials 

• Monthly Target: 4–7+ closed deals 

• Top Closers: 10–15+ deals/month 

If you can’t operate at this level, this role will not work for you.

 

COMPENSATION STRUCTURE

Base Salary: $300/month

Per Deal (Resets Monthly):

• Deal #1 — $200 

• Deal #2 — $250 

• Deal #3 — $300 

• Deal #4+ — $350 per deal 

 

REAL INCOME BREAKDOWN (NO GUESSING)

We believe in transparency:

Average Performer (4–7 Deals)

???? $1,200 – $2,500+/month

Strong Performer (8–12 Deals)

???? $2,800 – $4,500+/month

Top Closer (12–15+ Deals)

???? $5,000 – $7,000+/month



No cap. No ceiling. No limit.

The more you close, the more you make.

 

WHO THIS IS FOR

This role is for you if:

• You have 1+ year of sales experience 

• You are confident speaking with strangers daily 

• You are comfortable with cold calling and rejection 

• You can stay disciplined without supervision 

• You are competitive and performance-driven 

• You want to build real income + real skillset 

• You take ownership and don’t make excuses 

 

WHO THIS IS NOT FOR

Do NOT apply if:

• You are afraid of cold calling 

• You need constant guidance or motivation 

• You struggle with consistency 

• You are looking for “easy money” 

• You cannot handle rejection or pressure 

• You want a relaxed or low-effort role 

This is a high-performance environment.

 

WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT

• You are selling AI — one of the fastest-growing markets globally 

• You will develop high-income closing skills 

• You get real volume (not 1–2 deals/month environments) 

• You are paid based on performance — not politics 

• Fully remote — work from anywhere 

• Clear path to becoming a top closer / team leader 

 

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

Within 30–60 days:

• You are consistently closing deals 

• You understand the product deeply 

• You control conversations confidently 

• You are earning above average 

Within 90 days:

• You are a top performer or on track to be one 

• You have built real sales discipline 

• You have increased your income significantly 

 

HIRING PROCESS (FAST)

We move quickly.

1. Apply 

2. Short screening 

3. Interview 

4. Decision within 24–48 hours 

???? We are onboarding immediately.

 

LIMITED SPOTS

We are only bringing on a small number of closers.

Not everyone gets in.

 

FINAL NOTE

If you are confident in your ability to:

• Outwork others 

• Handle pressure 

• Close deals consistently 

Then this is an opportunity to build real income in a real market.



Please complete your application using the form below:

title: Inside Sales Associate | Growth Path to Appointment Setter and Closer ( $4-$6/Hour + Commission )
description:

title: Experienced Part/Full-Time Bookkeeper /Accountant (Remote)
description:
Please Read Carefully



To be considered for this position, you must carefully follow all application instructions.



Applications that do not follow the instructions or appear to be AI-generated will not be considered.

A short video introduction is required as part of the application process.



Please read the Application Instructions section at the bottom before applying.



About the Role



We are a fire and security contracting and engineering company seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Bookkeeper / Staff Accountant to maintain accurate financial records, organize documentation, and support smooth business operations.



This role is ideal for someone who takes ownership of their work, values structure and accuracy, and wants to grow into a leadership role in finance.



This is more than a bookkeeping role. It is an opportunity to become a key financial operator inside a growing company.



Key Responsibilities



Financial Management

Manage QuickBooks Online, including creating and sending invoices, tracking payments, and reconciling accounts

Maintain accurate financial records and ensure books are CPA-ready for tax filing and review

Process invoices, unpaid invoices, estimates, and job documentation

Review job completion forms to ensure accurate billing

Operational Accounting Support

Upload invoices and track payments in Service Channel

Monitor receivables and follow up on unpaid invoices

Ensure financial records are complete and properly documented

Documentation & Organization

Organize and audit Google Drive files using standardized naming conventions

Ensure paperwork and documents are routed to the correct stakeholders

Maintain strict organizational standards across all financial documentation

Manage and track insurance documentation

Systems & Process Support

Help create and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

Track and maintain relevant KPIs

Support internal systems and documentation using Notion



Requirements



5+ years of bookkeeping or accounting experience

Expert proficiency in QuickBooks Online (multi-entity experience preferred)

Strong experience with Google Sheets or Excel (formulas, pivot tables, reporting)

Proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Drive, Gmail, Calendar)

Comfortable using Notion for documentation and task tracking

Familiarity with Zoho CRM is highly preferred

Experience with Service Channel is a plus

Strong written and spoken English communication skills

Experience creating SOP documentation

Ability to track and maintain KPIs and operational metrics



Personal Traits



Highly organized and detail-oriented

Reliable and proactive

Independent worker with strong problem-solving skills

Strong sense of ownership and accountability



Position Details



Location: Remote (work from anywhere)

Schedule Options

      Part-Time

20 hours per week

      Full-Time

40 hours per week



Candidates must be available during U.S. business hours (8:00 AM – 4:00 PM Eastern Time) and reachable during that window.



Growth & Career Path



We are building a long-term team, and the right candidate will have opportunities to grow within the organization.

Potential growth path includes:

Transition to full-time employment

Leadership opportunity as Head of Finance Department

Long-term role within a fast-growing, tech-enabled contracting and engineering company

This role is ideal for someone looking for more than just a job, but a long-term career with increasing responsibility.



Application Instructions (Read Carefully)



To apply for this role, you must complete all five steps below.



Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



Step 1

Write a short paragraph explaining why you believe you are a good fit for this role.



Step 2

Answer the following question in 2–3 sentences:



If you are given five invoices to process, but one of them is missing supporting documents, what would you do?



Step 3

Use the following subject line in your
 application:



Bookkeeper Application – [Your Name]



Step 4

At the very end of your application, include the exact phrase:



“I want to synergize with you.”



Step 5



Submit a 2-minute video explaining your experience in:



Bookkeeping



Staff accounting



Financial controlling



You may upload the video using Google Drive or Loom and include the link in your application.

title: Content Writer for SEO/Blog/YouTube
description:
Hi there,



I'm looking for someone to write SEO articles for a blog on my website, and that is able to convert these SEO articles into YouTube video scripts.



My business is a music education company, primarily focused on 1 on 1 guitar lessons, but expanding into the launch and sale of online courses.



Successful applicant must be able to work well on a team. Submitted articles and scripts will be treated as drafts. If the suggestions would decrease the likelihood of a search based video, or an SEO article showing up in search, you must be able to provide relevant points about that to team members. 



Looking to start someone at 8 hours per week, will increase hours as needed. Raises and bonuses available after first three months. Guitar lessons available to successful applicant as a bonus perk of the job as well.



Knowledge of music, and of the guitar, especially niche guitar spaces like fingerstyle/classical guitar is an asset, but not required for the position. When applying, please set the subject line of your reply to "I want to work for you [name]" and where it says [name] insert the last name of a classical guitar composer. Creative choices are more likely to catch my attention. 



Looking forward to hearing from you!

Troy





P.S. If you do not disclose when you use AI for script writing or article writing, you will be immediately terminated without notice. Using these tools is part of our work life now, but passing off copy and pasted responses from what you prompt as your own work is unacceptable.

title: Cloud & Endpoint Support Engineer (Tier 2)
description:
Role Overview

The Cloud & Endpoint Support Engineer (Tier 2) is responsible for day-to-day endpoint operations and Microsoft 365 support across multiple client environments. This is a hands-on role focused on consistent execution, endpoint hygiene, patching, troubleshooting, and clear documentation. 



Key Responsibilities 

• Administer Microsoft 365 and core Azure/Entra ID services (user/mailbox management, Conditional Access changes, retention and mailbox policies). 

• Configure and manage Intune, Autopilot, Group Policy, application deployments, and configuration baselines. 

• Execute scheduled Windows and third-party patching, including reporting and remediation. 

• Monitor and maintain endpoint security baselines and EDR/RMM agents (e.g., SentinelOne, Arctic Wolf, NinjaOne). 

• Troubleshoot endpoint, mail flow, DNS, authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and basic firewall issues. 

• Resolve Tier 2 escalations and collaborate with senior engineers for complex i
. 

• Maintain runbooks for onboarding/offboarding, device builds, patching, and routine maintenance. 

• Use PowerShell for basic automation, validation, and reporting. 

• Document all work clearly and accurately. 

• Serve as the primary, hands-on owner of scheduled maintenance windows—executing, validating, and documenting patching and maintenance activities—and perform emergency after-hours changes as required. 



Qualifications 

• 3–5 years of experience supporting Microsoft 365 and Windows endpoints. 

• Strong hands-on experience with Intune, Autopilot, Group Policy, and patching workflows. 

• Experience with EDR/RMM tools in a support capacity. 

• Solid troubleshooting and documentation skills. 

• Clear and professional communication skills.

• Process requests via telephone and

• Troubleshoot and resolve issues on the initial call when possible, utilizing the internal knowledge base, experience, and team resources

• Monitor the unassigned ticket queue and follow up on assigned tickets every 24 hours

• Identify and escalate tickets to TTI management or appropriate support groups when needed

• Document issues and troubleshooting steps concisely in the ticketing system

• Follow up on open issues with escalation groups to provide updates to the customer

• Basic support and troubleshooting, such as password resets, printer configurations, break/fix instructions, ticket routing, and escalation to Level 2 and Level 3 support groups

• Familiarity with common software installations (i.e., Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative Cloud, etc.) and successfully following documented steps

• Analyze information about the users' issues and determine the best way to resolve these issues

• Provide support on Level 2 and Level 3 issues where configuration solutions have already been documented



Technology knowledge and support experience required:



• High Proficiency in providing support and troubleshooting Windows 10 and macOS

• Microsoft 365: Active Directory, Outlook, Excel, Word

• ITSM Ticketing System: ServiceNow, Connectwise

• Collaboration Tools: Teams, Slack, or Zoom

• Contact Center / Call Center VOIP: 8x8, Cisco, Mitel



A plus to have:



• Microsoft 365 Admin Center

• Azure Active Directory

• Exchange Admin Center

• Endpoint Manager Admin Center



Requirements:



• Strong Customer Service Skills

• Excellent oral/written communication skills

• Thorough understanding of computers (both Windows & MAC OSX preferred)

• Experience with
 systems and a basic understanding of Active Directory & Network troubleshooting is required

• Support of enterprise applications (proprietary or otherwise) is desired

• One year of experience working on a help desk is required

Candidates must demonstrate the ability to create content aligned with modern advertising standards across platforms such as Meta, TikTok, and YouTube.

• Incorporate motion graphics, subtitles, and dynamic visuals

title: Social Media Coordinator / Executive Assistant

We are seeking an organized, self-directed individual to join as a part-time Social Media Coordinator and Executive Assistant. This role is remote and requires 15-20 hours per week. The ideal candidate will be highly skilled in social media content creation and management, while also providing crucial administrative support.



Role Details

Job Title: Social Media Coordinator / Executive Assistant (Part-Time)



Time Commitment: 15-20 hours per week (approximately 10-14 hours Social Media, 4-6 hours Executive Assistant)

Schedule: Must be available to start at 12 PM PST

Compensation: $400-$500/month

Start Date Target: Mid to late April 2026



Key Responsibilities



This role is split into two primary functions: Social Media Coordination (approx. 70%) and Executive Assistant support (approx. 30%). The coordinator will support 3 clients, 2 of which are potential clients.

), 
, and TikTok.

s are missed. Respond to routine
s on behalf of Leann, following established guidelines.

), TikTok, or 
.

 triage, invoice/payment tracking, scheduling, and calendar management.

title: Remote Sales Development Representative (SDR | BDR | Appointment Setter | Cold Caller)
description:
About StoryGen

StoryGen (storygen.agency) is a fast-growing B2B lead generation agency specializing in building and scaling outbound sales teams for our clients. Our expertise is rooted in cold calling, where our Business Development Representatives (BDRs) take the lead in initiating conversations, qualifying prospects, and setting up high-quality meetings that drive revenue.



We work with a wide range of clients—from high-growth startups to established companies—across industries such as SaaS, digital marketing, IT services, fintech, staffing, and consulting. In this role, your work will play a direct role in generating pipeline and contributing to client success.



The Opportunity

We’re hiring a BDRs who excels in outbound sales and is comfortable operating in a high-volume calling environment. You’ll execute structured outreach campaigns, follow proven frameworks, and consistently deliver qualified meetings.



This role is ideal for someone who is detail-oriented, disciplined, and experienced in cold prospecting.



Application Process

We use a skills-based hiring approach to ensure a fair and objective process.



To apply, please complete the assessment and submit the following in the same link:



- Your resume

- A link to your internet speed test

- A short Loom audio recording (guide questions will be provided in the same link)



Apply here:





Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next steps.



Key Responsibilities

- Make high-volume outbound calls to B2B decision-makers

- Follow established scripts, frameworks, and objection-handling techniques

- Maintain accurate and detailed records of all activities in the CRM

- Handle post-booking tasks such as confirmation
s, client introductions, and gifting links when needed

- Reconnect with warm leads to improve conversion rates

- Collaborate with clients in real time to ensure proper qualification and seamless handoffs

- Provide feedback on lead lists, messaging, and scripts to improve campaign performance

- Work closely with the team to maintain consistent pipeline activity

- Stay aligned with client messaging and share insights from live conversations



What We’re Looking For

- Minimum of 2 years experience in a B2B SDR/BDR role focused on cold calling

- Track record of hitting or exceeding call and meeting targets

- Strong attention to detail, especially in CRM management

- Confident in handling objections and building rapport quickly

- Adaptable and able to learn new tools and workflows quickly

- Team player with strong collaboration skills

- Clear, professional communicator

- Comfortable working remotely in a fast-paced setup

- Familiarity with tools like HubSpot, Slack, Calendly, Coda, and Nooks (or similar) is a plus



Compensation & Benefits

- USD 1,000 Base Pay 

- Uncapped commission per qualified meetings held

- Fully remote work environment

- Monday to Friday schedule (8:30 AM – 6:00 PM EST)

- Opportunity to work across multiple B2B industries

- Career growth path into leadership roles

- Supportive, results-driven team culture

title: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
description:
We are seeking a strategic, hands-on Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to join our executive leadership team during a pivotal stage of growth. This role is ideal for a finance leader with a proven track record in startup environments—someone who has successfully guided companies through capital raises, investor relations, financial scaling, and the due diligence processes associated with mergers, acquisitions, or exits in the United States.



The CFO will serve as a trusted partner to the CEO, providing financial leadership, operational insight, and disciplined execution while supporting rapid growth and long-term value creation.



Key Responsibilities



Strategic & Executive Leadership



Act as a core member of the executive leadership team, advising the CEO on financial strategy, growth planning, and risk management



Develop and execute financial strategies aligned with company objectives and long-term exit or liquidity planning



Translate business strategy into financial models, forecasts, and actionable insights



Fundraising & Investor Relations



Lead all fundraising activities, including venture capital, private equity, strategic investors, and debt financing



Prepare investor materials including pitch decks, financial models, and data rooms



Manage ongoing investor relations, reporting, and board communications



Support valuation strategy and capital structure optimization



Financial Operations & Controls



Oversee all financial functions including accounting, FP&A, treasury, tax, payroll, and compliance



Establish scalable financial systems, processes, and internal controls suitable for a high-growth startup



Ensure compliance with U.S. GAAP, regulatory requirements, and best practices



Manage cash flow, runway planning, and capital allocation



M&A, Due Diligence & Exit Readiness



Lead financial due diligence for acquisitions, strategic partnerships, or company sale processes



Prepare and manage data rooms, financial disclosures, and responses to investor or buyer inquiries



Coordinate with legal, tax, and external advisors throughout transaction processes



Ensure the company is “exit-ready” from a financial, operational, and governance perspective



Team & Culture



Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing finance team



Foster a culture of accountability, transparency, and financial discipline



Partner cross-functionally with operations, sales, product, and legal teams



Qualifications



Required



Proven experience as CFO, VP of Finance, or equivalent senior finance role in startup or high-growth environments



Demonstrated success raising capital from investors in the United States



Hands-on experience leading financial due diligence for M&A, sale, or exit transactions



Strong knowledge of U.S. GAAP, corporate finance, and financial modeling



Experience working closely with Boards, investors, and executive leadership



Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field



Preferred



CPA, CFA, or MBA strongly preferred



Experience scaling companies from early-stage through growth or exit



Background in technology, SaaS, or innovation-driven businesses (industry flexible)



Prior experience working with PE, VC, or strategic buyers

title: Web QA & Delivery Coordinator
description:
Web QA & Delivery Coordinator

Remote · Full-time or Part-time Retainer ·



Location: Fully remote — work from anywhere in the Philippines

Budget: $1,200–$1,800/mo USD (approx. ?71,650–?107,475/mo) depending on experience

Hours: UK business hours (9am–5:30pm GMT/BST), Monday to Friday

Start: ASAP



About Vovi Studio:



Vovi Studio is a design-led web studio delivering high-quality websites and digital experiences for clients worldwide. We work with a small, talented team and pride ourselves on clear communication, great craft, and reliable delivery.

We're growing our retainer client base and need a reliable, detail-oriented coordinator to help us manage ongoing web maintenance and QA across our client projects.



The Role:



This is a hands-on delivery role. You will be the person who reviews live and staging websites, catches issues before clients do, writes clear tickets for developers, and keeps maintenance requests moving from inbox to done.

You won't just be coordinating — you'll be doing the QA yourself, which means reviewing websites in a browser, documenting bugs and improvements clearly, and following up to make sure fixes are completed correctly.



What You'll Do:

- Quality Assurance

- Review websites (desktop and mobile) against design references or brief

- Write clear, actionable bug reports and improvement notes for developers

- Use Loom or screenshots to document visual issues

- Re-test fixes and confirm when issues are resolved

- Maintain a QA checklist for repeatable testing across projects

- Maintenance Coordination

- Receive and triage incoming client maintenance requests

- Brief developers clearly with all context and assets needed

- Track the status of open requests and chase completion

- Update clients on progress in a friendly, professional tone

- Flag anything that's out of scope or needs a quote



What We're Looking For:



Must-haves:

3–5 years experience in a web agency, studio, or digital production environment

Experience writing QA tickets, bug reports, or dev briefs

Comfortable reviewing WordPress and/or Webflow websites

Able to read Figma files to compare designs against live builds

Strong written English — clear, concise, professional

Reliable, self-directed, and comfortable working async



Nice to haves:

Experience with staging environments (e.g. WP Engine, Kinsta, Webflow staging)

Familiar with project tools like Notion, Asana, Linear, or similar

Comfortable recording Loom walkthroughs of issues

Has worked with retainer or maintenance clients before



How to Apply

Send us a short message (no lengthy cover letters needed) with:

A brief intro — who you are and what you've been working on

Examples of QA notes, bug reports, or dev briefs you've written (screenshots or documents welcome)

Your available hours and preferred retainer rate in USD



We value clear, direct communication — your application is our first impression of how you'll write tickets and updates, so keep it sharp and to the point.

We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Admin VA (HealthCare)
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly experienced and dependable Admin Assistant (VA) to support the daily administrative and operational functions of our home care agency.

This role requires prior hands-on experience working within a home care or home health setting. Candidates must have a strong background in caregiver scheduling and recruitment, with supporting roles such as CNAs, HHAs, caregivers, and nurses.



The selected candidate will be part of our Virtual Assistant (VA) pool, serving as a critical back-office support resource for our internal team. This position plays a key role in ensuring seamless operations, including stepping in to cover responsibilities when another VA is on leave, maintaining uninterrupted workflow and service delivery.

This is a fast-paced, operations-driven role requiring excellent communication, organization, and the ability to manage staffing and administrative needs proactively.



Key Responsibilities



Provide consistent back-office administrative support to the internal operations team

Manage and assist with caregiver scheduling, ensuring proper shift coverage and real-time coordination with staff

Support recruitment efforts for healthcare professionals, including CNAs, HHAs, caregivers, and nurses

Professionally handle inbound and outbound calls,
s, and internal communications

Maintain accurate records, reports, and operational documentation

Assist in creating basic graphic materials (e.g., hiring posts, internal announcements) when needed

Serve as a backup VA within the pool, taking over the responsibilities of tea
mbers on leave to ensure continuity of operations

Support day-to-day operational workflows and assist leadership with urgent staffing or administrative needs



Qualifications



Required:

Mandatory experience working in a home care or home health agency setting

Proven experience in caregiver scheduling and healthcare recruitment

Experience supporting roles such as CNAs, HHAs, caregivers, and/or nurses

Strong background in phone handling and professional
 communication

Willingness to function as part of a VA pool and provide coverage when needed



Preferred:

Experience using home care scheduling systems or CRM platforms

Familiarity with tools such as Canva for basic graphic design



Work Setup

100% Remote

Must have a reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional workspace

Must be available during designated business hours and flexible to support urgent operational needs and VA coverage requirements



Application Requirement



INCLUDE YOUR UPDATED RESUME WITH YOUR APPLICATION.

Only candidates with relevant home care/home health experience in scheduling and recruitment will be considered.
